seoTitle: Four Corners vs Four Mile Creek — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Four Corners and Four Mile Creek. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Four Corners vs Four Mile Creek: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Four Corners and Four Mile Creek.
heading: Four Corners vs Four Mile Creek — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Four Corners, New South Wales and Four Mile Creek, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Four Corners has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to Four Mile Creek (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Four Corners or Four Mile Creek safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Four Corners has a flood risk score of 21/100, while Four Mile Creek scores 39/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Four Corners scores 20/100 for bushfire risk. Four Mile Creek scores 31/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Four Corners or Four Mile Creek?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Four Corners has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
